info.name: Parallel Programming API
info.description: Postman collection for testing all current API routes in the Laravel project. Commerce tables for carts, orders, and inventory movements exist in the database, but no API routes expose them yet.

request.description: Quick health check for the API root.
Creates a fresh admin user and saves the returned sanctum token into {{admin_token}}.
Logs in with the last generated admin credentials and refreshes {{admin_token}}.
Creates a fresh normal user and saves the returned sanctum token into {{user_token}}.
Logs in with the last generated normal user credentials and refreshes {{user_token}}.
Logs out the current admin token.
Logs out the current normal-user token.

exec: pm.test('Products list is an array', function () {

request.description: Returns all active products. If {{product_id}} is empty, it stores the first product id.

exec: pm.test('Most ordered list is an array', function () {

request.description: Returns top ordered active products.

exec: pm.test('Returned product has matching id', function () {

request.description: Returns one active product by id. Run Create Product or List Active Products first so {{product_id}} exists.
Creates a cart for the currently authenticated user and stores the id in {{cart_id}}.

exec: pm.test('Carts list is an array', function () {

request.description: Lists the authenticated user's carts.

exec: pm.test('Returned cart has matching id', function () {

request.description: Shows one cart for the authenticated user.
Updates the selected cart.
Deletes the selected cart.
Creates an item inside {{cart_id}}. Run Create Cart and Create Product first.

exec: pm.test('Cart items list is an array', function () {

request.description: Lists all cart items owned by the authenticated user.

exec: pm.test('Returned cart item has matching id', function () {

request.description: Shows one cart item for the authenticated user.
Updates the selected cart item and recalculates subtotal/cart totals.
Deletes the selected cart item.
Creates an order for the authenticated user and stores {{order_id}} and {{order_number}}.

exec: pm.test('Orders list is an array', function () {

request.description: Lists the authenticated user's orders.

exec: pm.test('Returned order has matching id', function () {

request.description: Shows one order for the authenticated user.
Updates the selected order.
Deletes the selected order.
Creates an item inside {{order_id}}. Run Create Order and Create Product first.

exec: pm.test('Order items list is an array', function () {

request.description: Lists all order items owned by the authenticated user.

exec: pm.test('Returned order item has matching id', function () {

request.description: Shows one order item for the authenticated user.
Updates the selected order item and recalculates subtotal/order totals.
Deletes the selected order item.
Creates an inventory movement as admin. Run Create Product first.

exec: pm.test('Inventory movements list is an array', function () {

request.description: Lists all inventory movement records for admins.

exec: pm.test('Returned inventory movement has matching id', function () {

request.description: Shows one inventory movement record.
Updates the selected inventory movement.
Deletes the selected inventory movement.
Creates a product as admin using multipart form-data with uploaded image files and stores the new id in {{product_id}}.
Updates the product metadata using multipart form-data. The request uses Laravel method spoofing so file uploads work reliably in Postman.
Updates stock quantity using the locked quantity endpoint.
Deletes the currently selected product id.
